title: Use robust race-relative pace and abnormal-regime features
summary: LapTime has extreme values above 2000 seconds, which likely reflect red flags, safety cars, pit/in-lap artifacts, or timing anomalies.
rationale: LapTime has extreme values above 2000 seconds, which likely reflect red flags, safety cars, pit/in-lap artifacts, or timing anomalies. Strategy research emphasizes that yellow flags and race state change pit-loss economics, so raw seconds can mislead unless converted to robust relative signals.
implementation_hint: Within Race_Year+LapNumber compute robust ranks/z-scores for LapTime, LapTime_Delta, degradation, and position change using median/MAD or quantiles; add extreme_lap, field_slowdown_rank, isolated_driver_slowdown, and winsorized versions of raw timing columns. Test clipping raw LapTime_Delta while keeping anomaly flags.
expected_effect: Should reduce overfit to timing outliers while preserving high-value safety-car/red-flag/pit-window signals.
risk: Outliers may already encode real stops; excessive clipping can remove useful signal unless anomaly flags are retained.
